title: Membrane Tube Image Analysis
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"a2drb","text":"Materials: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5amsn","text":"Image stack of multichannel confocal fluorescence images","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"72ubj","text":" FIJI  ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"92bme","text":"Tube enrichment quantification script (Python)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: In FIJI, create new image stacks from two rectangular selections: one containing a section of the membrane tube, and one containing the approximately horizontal section of the guv.
Save these new stacks as .tif files and paste paths into enrichment quantification script.
Run enrichment quantification, adjusting parameters such as ktub to fit your experimental conditions.
creator: Liv Jensen
